👗 Outfit inspirado en 1987 (estilo 80s auténtico)

Parte de arriba:
 • Blusa oversize con hombros marcados (hombreras grandes).
 • También funciona una camiseta gráfica vintage con logo retro.

Parte de abajo:
 • Falda de tubo brillante o leggings metalizados.
 • O jeans mom fit de tiro alto.

Calzado:
 • Tacones puntiagudos o tenis blancos chunky ochenteros.

Accesorios:
 • Aretes grandes tipo aro.
 • Cinturón ancho.
 • Lentes de sol estilo aviador.
 • Cabello con mucho volumen o cola alta con scrunchie.
Paleta: fucsia, morado, turquesa, dorado y negro.                   #Outfit1987 #Estilo80s #Aesthetic80s #VintageVibes #RetroFit

This iconic gym scene from Perfect features
Jamie Lee Curtis as Jessie Wilson, a charismatic aerobics instructor, and John Travolta as Adam Lawrence, a Rolling Stone journalist investigating the '80s fitness craze.
The tension between them turns a simple workout into one of the most flirtatious and memorable dance like exercise sequences ever filmed. Their chemistry, energy, and synchronized moves made this scene a cult favorite for fans of vintage fitness culture and romantic comedies alike.

Welcome to my Miami Tropical Deco powder room 🪞🌴

This little space is pure 80s Miami nostalgia for me. 

If you’re trying to hunt down similar pieces on Facebook Marketplace, Etsy, or eBay, here are the exact keyword clusters I use when sourcing:

• Brass Vanity
brass bathroom vanity | vintage brass sink | Hollywood Regency vanity | 1980s brass bathroom | retro brass furniture

• Art Deco Revival Vases
art deco revival vase | pastel art deco vase | 80s ceramic vase | Haeger style vase | deco shell vase

• Wicker + Rattan Accents
wicker shelving | vintage rattan shelf | tropical wicker decor | boho wicker accent | 80s coastal wicker

• Miami-Inspired Prints & Wall Art
Miami art deco print | Ocean Drive wall art | pastel Miami illustration | vintage Florida art | tropical deco print

( I love all of mine from @Chicago Wedding Photographer  on Etsy [ MahaStudios] ✨)

• Bath + Vanity Styling
@Vacation Inc. for all things 80’s/80’s nostalgic, beachy, and perfectly on-theme. Their products fit so effortlessly 🌞

This room is a small space, but it packs in everything I love about Miami Tropical Deco. Layered, nostalgic, and personal, not perfect.

I’m building a free sourcing guide with all my go-to search terms, tips, and places I hunt for vintage. If that sounds helpful, head to KaylaSueStyled.com and sign up so you don’t miss it.

✨ A MODA DOS ANOS 80: O IMPACTO DA MÚSICA NO ESTILO! 🎶👗

Os anos 80 foram uma explosão de cores vibrantes, cortes ousados e uma moda que refletia liberdade e futurismo. Mas você sabia que os artistas e bandas dessa época foram os maiores influenciadores do estilo que dominou a década? 🎸💃

🔥 Do New Wave ao estilo andrógino, a música ditava o que era tendência!

O movimento New Wave surgiu misturando o punk com a música eletrônica, trocando guitarras por sintetizadores. Bandas como Depeche Mode, Human League e Tears for Fears marcaram a estética sonora e visual, enquanto no Brasil grupos como Gang 90, Metrô e RPM trouxeram essa revolução para cá.

🎥 Filmes icônicos como Blade Runner e Guerra nas Estrelas também influenciaram a moda da época, trazendo um ar futurista às roupas e acessórios. O resultado? Muito neon, jaquetas metalizadas, ombreiras exageradas e penteados inesquecíveis!

🔮 E o estilo andrógino? Boy George, do Culture Club, desafiou padrões e inspirou um visual sem gênero, um reflexo da ousadia e inovação da década.

💥 Os anos 80 foram uma era onde a moda era expressão pura! Pôsteres nas paredes, roupas inspiradas nos ídolos e uma identidade visual única que até hoje impacta as tendências.

👉 E você? Qual banda dos anos 80 marcou seu estilo? Deixe nos comentários! ⬇️🎤👗

POV: 🌴✨ It’s 1985 in Miami and you’re living a lavish lifestyle.
Neon lights glowing off the ocean, palm trees swaying in the heat, and the whole city buzzing like it never sleeps.
The music? Synths, drum machines, sax solos — everything had a heartbeat. 🎶🔥
The cars? Boxy, bold, chrome shining everywhere — Corvettes, Testarossas, big bodies cruising down Ocean Drive like they owned the night. 🚗💨
The people? Confident, stylish, sun-kissed… living fully in the moment because there was no social media, no scrolling, no comparing — just real life, real vibes, and real energy.
1985 Miami felt like freedom. Like magic. Like a movie you never wanted to end. 🌅🌴

#1980sMiami #80sVibes #Miami1985 #RetroLifestyle #VintageMiami

POV: you grew up in the 80s.

You didn’t know it at the time, but you were in the middle of something you’d spend years trying to get back to. Mornings came in slow. Sun through the blinds, TV warming up, the house not fully awake yet. Teddy Ruxpin talking like he had somewhere to take you. Jiffy Pop rattling on the stove. A cold Slice on the counter, a Summit bar gone before you even thought to save it. Nothing felt important… but everything felt like it belonged.

Afternoons stretched out without asking anything from you. Bikes in the yard, screen doors opening and closing, friends just showing up like they always did. And when night came, it felt bigger than it should’ve. Streetlights, quiet drives, music that hit before you understood why. You weren’t documenting it. You weren’t thinking about it. You were just in it. And if you grew up in the 80s… you didn’t just live it, you caught something most people never will.

Leather. Chains. Fishnets. Big hair that defies gravity.
The 80s didn’t whisper — they roared.
This wasn’t just fashion. It was armor. Oversized band tees. Studded belts pulled tight at the waist. High-waisted leather that caught every flash of red and blue light on the dance floor. Eyeliner heavy enough to survive a mosh pit.
You didn’t go to the club in the 80s to blend in. You went to be seen.
When the lights hit and that guitar riff starts, there’s no pretending. You either own the room… or you get swallowed by it.
Tonight she’s not background noise. She’s center stage energy. Chain in one hand. Confidence in the other.
Hair bigger. Music louder. Attitude unapologetic.
If you know… you know.
Drop a 🔥 if you would’ve survived the 80s club scene.

POV: You’re Back in Your 80s Room 🎧📼🕹 You just walked into the ultimate 1980s teenager’s bedroom—and it’s like stepping into a time machine. From the bright neon ‘RAD’ sign glowing above the lava lamp to the stacks of VHS tapes, this room has everything an 80s kid dreamed of. On the bed? A Sony Walkman with classic foam headphones, ready to blast Madonna, Prince, or Michael Jackson. The tube TV has the NES console fired up, controllers plugged in, ready for a round of Super Mario Bros.

Posters of 80s icons cover the walls, and that geometric comforter in hot pink, turquoise, and purple screams retro aesthetic. There’s a Trapper Keeper notebook open on the bed, full of doodles and mixtape playlists. The boombox is sitting on the floor next to a fresh pair of Reebok sneakers, and the sunlight filtering through the blinds casts those iconic striped shadows across the room.

If you grew up in the 80s, this scene hits different. And if you didn’t, you’re about to experience the vintage vibes we all loved. This isn’t just nostalgia—it’s an 80s aesthetic time capsule.
